response.justification: Bogdan is an experienced full-stack engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but the role requires deep Java and Kotlin expertise, Spring ecosystem experience, and genuine interest in JVM internals and IDE tooling. His resume lists Java as a skill but it appears only peripherally in his history (robotic arms tooling, mobile device orchestration at Altom), with no evidence of enterprise Java, Spring, Hibernate, Quarkus, or JVM-level work. His primary contributions across 15+ years are in web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js, D lang), and he is currently in a career gap. There is no alignment with the core technical focus areas of this role.
strengths: Long career with demonstrated software craftsmanship principles (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing)
Some exposure to developer tooling (MCP code generators, test runners, CI infrastructure) showing interest in the meta-layer of software
Experience with large-scale, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby) indicating systems-level thinking
concerns: No demonstrable Java or Kotlin expertise at a senior level — primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/D lang
Zero mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework across the entire employment history
No IDE internals, language tooling, or JVM-specific experience relevant to building an enterprise IDE product
